title: 5 Tips for Safe Land Buying in Kenya
excerpt: Buying land is a big investment. Here is how to ensure your transaction is safe and legal.
content:
### Introduction
Buying land in Kenya is an exciting milestone, but it requires careful due diligence to avoid scams and legal pitfalls.

### 1. Verification of Title Deeds
Always perform an official search at the Ministry of Lands. This confirms the current legal owner and whether there are any encumbrances (loans or disputes) on the property.

### 2. Use Verified Professionals
Ensure you work with EARB registered agents and LSK registered lawyers. PlotPesa verifies these professionals so you don't have to.

### 3. Conduct a Site Visit
Never buy land without seeing it. A physical visit helps confirm the boundaries and the actual state of the neighborhood.

### 4. Survey Confirmation
Involve a surveyor to confirm the plot matches the deed plan. This prevents encroachment issues later.

title: Why Surveying is Critical for Your Plot
excerpt: Discover why a professional survey is the most important step before fencing or building.
content:
### The Importance of Boundaries
Boundary disputes are among the most common legal battles in Kenya. A professional surveyor ensures your property is accurately marked based on the official survey maps.

### Beaconing
Beacons are the physical markers of your land. If they are missing or moved, only a surveyor can re-establish them legally.

### Subdivision and Mutations
If you are part of a larger scheme, ensure the mutation forms are correctly filed and the subdivision is approved by the county government.
